mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth remains positive and above historical median.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 132,821k suggests continued labor market strength
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where month-over-month change is positive, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.50% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth below 0.3%, the real interest rate is negative, signaling monetary accommodation that compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive forward returns. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when both conditions hold simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% and CPIAUCSL at 229.098 suggest accommodative stance
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (both thresholds met) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2.0% signals economic deceleration that prompts equity market repricing downward as earnings growth expectations contract. This regime-conditional mechanism is most powerful in late-cycle expansions where growth deceleration is unexpected.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,429.0B as of 6 months prior suggests need for recent growth assessment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and margin compression for cyclical firms. When INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average, equity valuations contract as investors reduce earnings forecasts for manufacturing-sensitive sectors over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 96.2095 as of 2 months prior
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move together as joint indicators of consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, discretionary spending and construction activity signal robust aggregate demand, driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both demand channels to confirm.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 407,805M and HOUST at 698k as of 2 months prior
regime_hint: expansion phase with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which depresses equity valuations as earnings expectations decline. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 60-day returns when unemployment acceleration exceeds this threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.2% as of 1 month prior
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 1.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable (month-over-month growth < 0.2%), the Fed is tightening in a disinflationary environment. This regime-conditional mechanism signals negative 20-day returns as rising real rates compress equity valuations, particularly in growth-sensitive sectors.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both accelerate month-over-month simultaneously, signaling synchronized supply and labor capacity expansion. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 60-day returns as firms increase production and hiring in tandem, indicating strong underlying economic momentum and earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show recent positive levels suggesting capacity utilization
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS show positive month-over-month growth, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
